===============================================

Today is February 10, 2026. Regarding the disappearance of Nancy Guthrie, what specific action did the FBI observe the masked individual performing in the newly recovered surveillance footage?
(A) Picking up vegetation from the ground to cover the camera lens
(B) Using a specialized tool to disconnect the doorbell's power supply
(C) Spraying a dark substance directly onto the lens of the Nest camera
(D) Removing the recording device from the door frame entirely
(E) Placing a piece of adhesive tape over the motion sensor
explanation: According to the article, the FBI released videos showing the person approach the front door, check the camera, and then pick up "some vegetation from the ground and using it to cover the camera lens."
model_output:
Based on web searches and detailed page browses related to the Nancy Guthrie disappearance case, the FBI-released surveillance footage from February 10, 2026, shows a masked individual tampering with the doorbell camera at her Tucson home. Specifically, the footage depicts the person covering the lens with a gloved hand and then using vegetation (described variously as a shrub, flowers, or part of a plant) pulled or ripped from the nearby yard or garden to further obscure the camera[[1]](https://www.newsnationnow.com/missing/police-surveillance-photo-nancy-guthrie-reports)[[2]](https://nypost.com/2026/02/10/us-news/nancy-guthrie-investigators-to-release-photo-of-subject-tied-to-her-mysterious-disappearance-nbc-news-reports)[[3]](https://ktla.com/news/nationworld/nancy-guthrie-disappearance-latest). This aligns most closely with option (A), as it involves picking up (or ripping/pulling) vegetation from the ground/yard to cover the lens. Other options, such as spraying a substance or using tape, do not match the reported details.

<final_answer>
(A)
</final_answer>

CITATIONS:
- NewsNation ([https://www.newsnationnow.com/missing/police-surveillance-photo-nancy-guthrie-reports])
- New York Post ([https://nypost.com/2026/02/10/us-news/nancy-guthrie-investigators-to-release-photo-of-subject-tied-to-her-mysterious-disappearance-nbc-news-reports])
- KTLA ([https://ktla.com/news/nationworld/nancy-guthrie-disappearance-latest])

===============================================

Today is February 10, 2026. During the congressional hearing regarding the deaths of Alex Pretti and Renee Good, how many Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) agents were confirmed to have already received their body-worn cameras?
(A) 6,000
(B) 3,000
(C) 10,000
(D) 13,000
(E) 20,000
explanation: Todd Lyons, the acting head of ICE, stated during the hearing that "more than 3,000 ICE officers have received their cameras."
model_output:
Based on the web search results and detailed article content, the congressional hearing in question took place on February 10, 2026, before the House Homeland Security Committee. It addressed the January 2026 deaths of U.S. citizens Renee Good and Alex Pretti during ICE operations in Minneapolis, Minnesota. During the hearing, ICE Acting Director Todd Lyons testified about the agency's body-worn camera program. Specifically, he confirmed that 3,000 out of approximately 13,000 ICE agents had already received and were wearing body cameras at that time[[1]](https://www.foxnews.com/politics/ice-director-says-minnesota-bodycam-footage-will-be-released-amid-transparency-push)[[2]](https://www.latimes.com/politics/story/2026-02-10/immigration-officials-grilled-over-u-s-citizen-deaths-during-oversight-hearing). (An additional 6,000 cameras were noted as being in the process of deployment, but those had not yet been received by agents.)

This matches option (B).

<final_answer>
(B)
</final_answer>

CITATIONS:
- [Fox News] (https://www.foxnews.com/politics/ice-director-says-minnesota-bodycam-footage-will-be-released-amid-transparency-push)
- [Los Angeles Times] (https://www.latimes.com/politics/story/2026-02-10/immigration-officials-grilled-over-u-s-citizen-deaths-during-oversight-hearing)

===============================================

Today is February 10, 2026. According to the Commerce Department report released on Tuesday, what was the month-over-month percentage change in US retail sales for November 2025?
(A) 2.4%
(B) 3.3%
(C) 0.6%
(D) 0.7%
(E) 0.9%
explanation: The article states that retail sales were unchanged in December "after a 0.6% increase in November."
model_output:
The Commerce Department report released on Tuesday, February 10, 2026, was the delayed Advance Monthly Sales for Retail and Food Services for December 2025[[1]](https://www.census.gov/retail/sales.html). According to the details in this report, the month-over-month percentage change for US retail sales in November 2025 (i.e., from October to November) was an increase of 0.6%[[2]](https://www.census.gov/retail/marts/www/marts_current.pdf). While the report revised the October 2025 figure downward, it did not indicate any revision to the November 2025 figure from the previously reported 0.6%[[3]](https://www.reuters.com/business/retail-consumer/us-retail-sales-beat-expectations-november-2026-01-14).

<final_answer>
(C)
</final_answer>

CITATIONS:
- Census Bureau Monthly Retail Trade Sales Report (https://www.census.gov/retail/sales.html)
- Census Bureau Advance Monthly Sales PDF (https://www.census.gov/retail/marts/www/marts_current.pdf)
- Reuters (https://www.reuters.com/business/retail-consumer/us-retail-sales-beat-expectations-november-2026-01-14)

explanation: The article states: "It is estimated to have cost $6.4bn CAD (£3.4bn)."
model_output:
Based on recent reports and updates about the Gordie Howe International Bridge project, the estimated total cost has risen due to delays and other factors. Multiple sources, including official reports and news articles, confirm the current figure.

The estimated cost is $6.4bn CAD[[1]](https://www.ctvnews.ca/windsor/article/fact-check-canada-paid-for-gordie-howe-bridge-but-doesnt-own-it-alone)[[2]](https://www.cbc.ca/news/canada/windsor/windsor-gordie-howe-trump-bridge-opening-truth-social-post-9.7081929)[[3]](https://planetdetroit.org/2025/09/detroit-bridge-community-impact)[[4]](https://gordiehoweinternationalbridge.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/10/Annual-Report-2024-2025-2025-06-30-FINAL-English-for-web.pdf).

<final_answer>
(B)
</final_answer>

Today is February 10, 2026. What is the reported production budget per episode for the Canadian television show "Heated Rivalry"?
(A) C$10m
(B) C$3.6m
(C) C$2.6m
(D) C$5m
(E) C$1.5m
explanation: The article states the show was "produced in Canada on a minimal budget - reportedly less than C$5m ($3.6m; £2.6m) per episode."
model_output:
The production budget per episode for the Canadian TV show "Heated Rivalry" is reported in various sources with some conflicts. The BBC and several syndicated articles indicate it is less than C$5m (equivalent to $3.6m USD or £2.6m GBP) per episode. Other sources, including a podcast interview with producer Brendan Brady and the Wikipedia entry, specify it as just under C$3m per episode (equivalent to about $2.2m USD). Some earlier rumors or reports list total production costs around C$11-12m for the 6-episode season, which would be about C$1.83-2m per episode. There are also conflicting conversions and ranges like $3-5m per episode (likely USD, equating to roughly C$4.2-6.9m).

The most consistent recent and authoritative sources (the producer's interview and Wikipedia) point to just under C$3m per episode, but since that is not an exact option, the closest match is C$2.6m, which also appears as the GBP equivalent in many reports and is closer to the confirmed figure than C$3.6m.

<final_answer>
(C)
</final_answer>

CITATIONS:
- BBC [web:0] (https://www.bbc.com/news/articles/c3edk87vqljo)
- LA Times [web:9] (https://www.latimes.com/entertainment-arts/awards/story/2026-02-03/how-heated-rivalry-changed-game-for-canadian-tv)
- Wikipedia [web:54] (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Heated_Rivalry)
- Podscripts transcript [web:35] (https://podscripts.co/podcasts/pivot/how-did-heated-rivalrys-producers-make-their-massive-hit)
- The Stylus [web:66] (https://thestylus.org/7523/entertainment/low-budget-tv-series-heated-rivalry-takes-the-world-by-storm)

Today is February 10, 2026. In what year was the ban on indigenous potlatch gatherings officially lifted in Canada?
(A) 1970
(B) 1914
(C) 1800
(D) 2015
(E) 1951
explanation: The article states: "The ban was lifted in 1951, and in 2015 Canada's Truth and Reconciliation Commission found that it was part of a 'cultural genocide'..."
model_output:
The potlatch is a traditional ceremonial gathering among certain Indigenous peoples in Canada, particularly in the Pacific Northwest, involving feasting, gift-giving, and cultural practices. Historical records indicate that the Canadian government imposed a ban on potlatch ceremonies as part of efforts to suppress Indigenous cultures, with the ban enacted in the late 19th century (around 1884 or 1885, depending on the source). Based on reliable sources, this ban was officially lifted in 1951[[1]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Potlatch_ban)[[2]](https://kumugwe.ca/potlatch-67-67)[[3]](https://www.cbc.ca/news/indigenous/historical-ban-spirituality-felt-indigenous-women-today-1.4036528).

<final_answer>
(E)
</final_answer>

(B) 1990
(C) 2008
(D) 2004
(E) 2019
explanation: The article states: "the president and the White House have repeatedly said he was in the dark about Epstein's crimes before he broke off contact with him in around 2004..."
model_output:
I reviewed various sources, including recent news articles, Wikipedia timelines, and statements from Trump and the White House. The consistent claim from President Trump and the White House is that contact with Epstein ended around 2004, following a falling out over a real estate bidding war. This aligns with Trump's 2019 statement that he hadn't spoken to Epstein in 15 years (pointing to 2004) and more recent 2026 references to the break occurring "more than 20 years ago" (again, 2004 from a 2026 perspective). Recent revelations about a possible 2006 call from Trump to a police chief (not to Epstein) are downplayed by the White House, with no change to their stated timeline.

<final_answer>
(D)
</final_answer>
